# Study Workspace

Eleven note-grounded tools assembled by `buildWorkspaceNoteBundle()`. The
workspace is the primary surface for studying a single concept — a left
"lesson" pane (note-derived steps, optionally regenerated by the LLM) and
a right "tool" pane that hosts the active tool.

## Source gate

`hasSource` = analyzed uploads whose `extractedText` is at least 80 characters
(course-scoped when `courseId` is set on the workspace).

Without source: every tool renders an empty state with an upload CTA — no
demo content is ever shown unless `Settings → Demo showcase` is explicitly
enabled.

## Tools

| Tab | Module | Input | Output |
| --- | ------ | ----- | ------ |
| Concept map | `buildConceptMapFromCourse` | topics, glossary, **PMI co-occurrence over sentence windows** | nodes + prerequisite + related edges (citations recorded) |
| Sandbox | `notesSupportEconomicsSandbox` / `sandboxInsightFromNotes` | concept-relevant excerpt | sliders + insight summarized via biased TextRank |
| Leitner | `buildFlashcards` | glossary, definitions, summarized excerpts | flashcard deck; **FSRS-4 ratings → store** via `submitLeitnerRating` |
| Compare | `extractComparisons` | Markdown tables → "X vs Y" patterns → glossary fallback | three-tier comparison rows |
| Whiteboard | `StudyWhiteboard` | formulas extracted from notes | local canvas strokes (scoped per task) |
| Feynman | `buildFeynmanOutline` + `feynmanRubric` | topic objectives, keyphrases, glossary | outline + gap hints + offline rubric (subject-agnostic) |
| Timer | `StudyTimer` | — | session minutes logged to activity stream |
| Debate | `buildDebateTreeFromNotes` | sentence scoring on claim/support/refute axes | argument tree with separate support / counter subtrees |
| Reader | `relevantExcerpt` + **`documentStructure`** + **`readerDocumentLayout`** | concept-scored BM25 excerpt; **`sourceFullText`** full document | structured `<h3>` sections, `<p>` paragraphs, `<ul>` lists (enumerated syllabus rows, multi-line items); adaptive PDF line-wrap reconstruction; section nav chips |
| Scratchpad | `extractFormulas` + `formulaSolver` | formulas in notes | generic shunting-yard solver (sin/cos/log/sqrt/etc.), per-task persistence |
| Source | annotations panel | `annotationText` | per-file highlights (local) |

## Source intelligence

Every grounded workspace session now computes a `sourceIntelligence` report in
`workspaceNoteContent.ts` and renders it above the lesson pane.

- **Inputs** β€” top BM25 passages (`topRelevantChunks`), concept relevance,
  explicit definitions, glossary overlap, worked examples, extracted formulas,
  comparison rows, concept-map size, step count, quiz availability,
  **`documentStructure`** (section kind, count, heading previews).
- **Output** β€” `score` (0β€“100), `band` (`weak` / `moderate` / `strong`),
  `bestTool`, a human-readable reason, strengths, gaps, and next-action hints.
- **Recommendation logic** β€” tool scoring is capped per signal family so a
  large comparison table cannot drown out formulas/practice or terminology.
- **UX effect** β€” the user sees whether the current concept is truly
  well-grounded, what is missing from the notes, detected **section chips**
  (conversation / FAQ / slides / headings), and can jump straight into the
  recommended tool via `SourceIntelligenceCard`.

## Step rail

`buildWorkspaceStepsFromNotes()` produces the lesson rail from the source:

- Sections from **`detectDocumentSections` on the full analyzed text** (not just
  the BM25 excerpt) when 2+ headings/turns are detected — each labeled
  Core Concept / Deep Dive / Key Insight / Practice.
- Otherwise, the top keyphrases from `rankKeyphrases` filtered by
  `conceptRelevanceScore > 0.1`.
- Otherwise, biased-TextRank sentence summaries.
- Always followed by a final Quiz step.

Semantic content per step is produced by `getNoteContentForLessonStep()` in
`groundedLesson.ts` — section-aware mapping for intro / explanation / example
/ misconception / practice / summary that picks the best matching section
rather than paragraph rotation, and uses biased TextRank with topic + concept
bias terms for the intro and summary.

## Quiz

`buildQuizFromNotes(text, concept, glossary, lang)` returns a single
`QuizDef` (cloze or MC):

- **Cloze branch** — picks the highest-relevance glossary term for the
  concept and blanks the term in its definition. Distractors are
  near-miss glossary terms (`rankDistractorTerms` over Jaccard token
  similarity).
- **MC branch** — picks the highest-relevance sentence from the source as
  the correct answer; distractors are near-miss glossary definitions and
  other source sentences (`rankDistractorSentences`).
- **Option ordering** — deterministic Fisher–Yates shuffle seeded by
  `(concept, correctAnswer)`. The correct answer is randomly placed but
  the order is stable across React re-renders.

## Persistence

All workspace state is scoped per task (`workspace:${taskId}`):

| Key | Module |
| --- | ------ |
| `synapse:workspace-progress` | current step index (`saveWorkspaceStep`) |
| `synapse:workspace-notes` | per-task scratch notes (`loadWorkspaceNotes`) |
| `synapse:concept-map-positions` | per-task concept-map node coordinates |
| `synapse:whiteboard-strokes` | per-task whiteboard strokes (legacy `synapse.whiteboard.v1` migrated automatically) |
| `synapse:scratchpad-formulas` | per-task scratchpad formulas, variables, steps |
| `debate-{progressKey}` | argument-map node positions |

See `PERSISTENCE.md` for the full key list.

## Keyboard shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action |
| -------- | ------ |
| `Esc` | Close overlays / workspace |
| `⌘K` / `Ctrl+K` | Open the **command palette** (works even while typing) |
| `←` / `→` | Previous / next lesson step |
| `1`…`9`, `0` | Switch to tool 1..10 (`0` selects the 10th tool) |
| `L` / `T` / `S` | Focus lesson / focus tool / split layout |
| `N` | Toggle session notes panel |
| `?` | Show the keyboard-shortcuts overlay |

The command palette (`src/components/workspace/CommandPalette.tsx`) groups
every action into Tools / Layout / Session and supports type-to-filter
(token-AND match across label + group + hint).

## Mobile

`StudyWorkspace.tsx` listens to `window.matchMedia('(max-width: 767px)')`
and switches to **single-pane mode** below that breakpoint. A top-bar
pane-swap button toggles between the lesson and tool views; the layout
shortcuts (`L`/`T`/`S`) all map to single-pane modes on mobile.

Below the `lg` breakpoint (1024px), the desktop `WorkspaceDock` hides and
a **fixed bottom bar** opens `WorkspaceMobileToolDrawer` — a bottom sheet with
clustered tools (SW-P1-02, `552d0ef`). Same tool groups as desktop dock
(`WORKSPACE_TOOL_GROUPS` from `workspaceToolRegistry`).

## Lesson generation (LLM)

When a proxy + LLM key are configured and `hasSource` is true,
`generateLessonPanels()` calls the proxy to produce a richer outline; if
the LLM is unavailable or returns malformed JSON, the workspace falls
back to the deterministic biased-TextRank summaries from `groundedLesson.ts`
(see `ALGORITHMS.md` §2.5).

## Correlation bus & discoverability

Every grounded session exposes a **discoverability panel** above the tool pane
(`workspace-discoverability`). It surfaces:

- **Correlation chips** — mastery, focus term, Leitner due count, IRT θ,
  spaced-step due count, sandbox sensitivity cue (all from `workspaceCorrelation`).
- **Per-tool feature guide** — shipped capabilities for the active tab (W0–W8),
  with quick actions (open reader focus, Leitner due, quiz jump, command palette).
- **Source intelligence** — grounded score and recommended tool when `hasSource`.

All tools read/write through `workspaceFocus` + `workspaceCorrelation` so
features stay harmonized (not isolated silos).

## Shipped upgrades (W0–W8)

| Phase | Highlights |
| ----- | ---------- |
| **W0–W2** | Focus bus, adaptive steps, Anki export, exam timer, PNG concept map, teacher annotations |
| **W3–W4** | Reader translation, compare CSV/sort, force layout, whiteboard layers, Feynman rubric export, debate counter-args |
| **W5** | Bilingual paragraph sync, annotation SSE/poll, spaced step scheduling, hierarchy layout |
| **W6** | Leitner heatmap/deck sync, scratchpad graph, timer .ics, paragraph TTS, annotation SSE |
| **W7** | Quiz IRT, sandbox sensitivity heatmap, LaTeX stamps, compare diff+CSV, command palette macros |
| **W8** | Multi-item quiz session + confidence rating; Feynman voice + auto-gap; debate rebuttal graph; concept-map collaborative cursors (SSE); reader OCR overlay |
| **Launch Wave 7** | SW-07 nextAction toolbar sync; SW-P1-02 mobile drawer; SW-P1-04 reader↔step; UTF-8 mojibake repair; Noto Greek typography (`552d0ef`) |

## Roadmap / next scale work

Shell UX (resizable split, pop-out tools, session continuity) and content-quality
dashboard remain in `EXHAUSTIVE_PRODUCT_SCALE_BLUEPRINT.md` §5. Infra deferrals
(BullMQ, pgvector, LTI) are tracked in `WORKSPACE_UPGRADE_PLAN.md`.

See [`ARCHITECTURE.md`](ARCHITECTURE.md) for data flow and
[`ALGORITHMS.md`](ALGORITHMS.md) for the algorithms behind every extractor.
